Dr. Sydney Brenner

personal site

Director of Research. He received degrees in Medicine and Science from the University of Witwatersrand, South Africa and D. Phil. in Chemistry from Oxford University in England. Dr. Brenner is known for his substantial contributions to genetics, including the identification of mRNA (with Francis Crick and Matthew Meselson), the elucidation that the genetic code consists of triplets (with Francis Crick), and the development of the nematode C. elegans as a model research organism. He was Director of the Unit of Molecular Genetics at the Medical Research Council in Cambridge, England, and is a Senior Fellow of King's College in Cambridge. Most recently, Dr. Brenner has been studying vertebrate gene and genome evolution. In the course of this work, he has developed new ways of analyzing gene sequences, which promise to give insight into embryonic development and evolution of the vertebrate lineage.
